item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice catch! I've edited the piece and opted for fix B. Thank you for noticing this and providing a quick solution!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A slight contradiction:
Third paragraph of the description says that everyone in the produced mementos (except the wallet-bearer) is deceased.
Then MCD test subject #17 got photos of his sister who is comatose but alive.

I see two possible fixes:
A) tweak the #17 entry to sisterly death after X years in a coma, or just streamline to death on impact.
B) tweak the description so that it reads "In all Foundation-controlled tests to date…" or similar phrasing. Which shows that the researchers noted case #17 and are attempting due diligence, but haven't yet recreated similar results.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I'm into it

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
I like it. Commence with the linking!

ETA: oh also I'll nudge Roget about this, since he's co-author. I think he'd be fine with it though.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1571 was initially recovered during a Foundation raid on a Marshall, Carter, and Dark Ltd facility, located in ██████, WV. During this raid, partial documentation regarding testing of SCP-1571's effects was discovered. These documents indicate that Marshall, Carter, and Dark Ltd had retrieved SCP-1571 as a donation, and were preparing to destroy it after it affected several of the organization's clients. As of 11/19/1986, SCP-1571 has been contained and classified as Safe.

This is the first mention of MC&D in the article, from what I can see, so it makes sense that this is where the link would be placed. Just make the first "Marshall, Carter, and Dark Ltd" a link.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Hmmm. Where would the link to the MC&D hub be?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at anytime.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

In the bit the first addendum, in which the letter mentions that some people go to MC&D for curios, and others go there for dangerous/exciting things, it would make sense to link to other MC&D articles that emphasize those qualities. SCP-604 could work as an example of an adventurous MC&D product, while SCP-209 may be a good example of a curio. Combined with a link to the MC&D hub, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

I regret to inform you that the work we've done on the wallet shows it simply isn't viable for auction. It doesn't fit our clientele. The ones looking for something dangerous and exciting will find none of that, and the ones looking for a curio will find it much less than remarkable. As we've had testers in the field for almost two years now, I am of the opinion that any clients who purchase the item would barely notice the effects.

To this:

I regret to inform you that the work we've done on the wallet shows it simply isn't viable for auction. It doesn't fit our clientele. The ones looking for something dangerous and exciting will find none of that, and the ones looking for a curio will find it much less than remarkable. As we've had testers in the field for almost two years now, I am of the opinion that any clients who purchase the item would barely notice the effects.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think there's probably some unexploited feels-potential in the containment procedures themselves. The wallet gives you a little keepsake of something that really matters to you… and then the Foundation requires you to put that keepsake in a locker at work and leave it there.

item.title: Re: Question...
item.content:encoded: For more about Amnesiac Treatment see SCP-002-J

item.title: Re: Question...
item.content:encoded:
Perhaps it would create some items based on past memories with pieces missing.

I'm not sure if fragmented memories would be regarded with as much sentimentality. One would hope that their most cherished memories are some of the ones they remember the most clearly (or so they think), right?

make a character realize there was something not right about his memory, when pictures of places dont match up with the space in which they were taken

Or it could just be the object's effect. Pictures (well, modern ones anyway) only capture a brief snapshot in time. Just because you weren't smiling in that one shot doesn't necessarily mean you had a terrible time at the wedding. It just means at the point in time when the picture was taken, you weren't smiling.

Or maybe you did have a terrible time at the wedding, but in retrospect it wasn't that bad. Who knows. Memories are weird.

item.title: Re: Question...
item.content:encoded: Mmmm, this might be interesting to explore in a tale. Where perhaps the foundation would allow someone that had a mind wipe posses the wallet for awhile as part of an experiment. Perhaps it would create some items based on past memories with pieces missing. Or it could be used as a "tool" in a story to make a character realize there was something not right about his memory, when pictures of places dont match up with the space in which they were taken, as a simple example. lmao by the way on being treated with an amnesiac, well played, but ya amnestics the drugs is is what i meant.

item.title: Re: Question...
item.content:encoded:
I'm assuming you meant "amnestics" since being treated with a person who has amnesia (an amnesiac) wouldn't really do anything because a person is not a medication

It depends on how long the person keeps it with them, and whether or not they're emotionally strained during that time. Given that the effects of the object are based on subjective memory, it's most likely that the memories affected by the amnestics would not trigger manifestation of items, but items would still be generated based on the memories that haven't been wiped.

item.title: Question...
item.content:encoded: What do you think would happen if someone that was treated with amnesiacs or had amnesia (sp) were to posses it? Do you think this could be a potential security risk or do you think it would simply produce nothing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i saw it was meh cheaply emotional on a superficial level, but all the minor details being "off" really disturbed me greatly and makes me think there is something else wrong with it, perhaps entirely tied to a person's own distorted memory. This alone makes me go "WTF" and upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The best icing is reminiscing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can I have one of these wallets, please?

Also…

When SCP-1571 is kept on the person of one subject continuously, it may begin to produce documents, small items, and photographs related to individuals close to the subject.

Was the exact spot where this earned my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How does one have a "trying too hard"?- Scantron

You've given me a Cheezburger idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I believe I got that, thanks!

I do understand where you're coming from on both points—those were the parts that were difficult for Roget and me to think through, and, the MC&D documentation was something that wasn't there at the initial conceptualization of the SCP. Admittedly, I can't say I'm sure how to fix the points you've addressed at the moment. Nevertheless, thank you for your critique.

also seriously everyone talks about the soup bowl so often d:

Darnit wiki syntax

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The bowl of soup and everything having to do with the "backroots" come immediately to mind. I am, for the record, not a big fan of the soup either.

And maybe 'tacked on' isn't the right word. I just look at this and, summing it up, it's a combination "MC&D's clients are all really awful human beings, in case you forgot" with a GoI doing all the testing for the Foundation. It's hard to convey things like "emotional importance" in a scientific article, and the addendum just doesn't really add anything because I don't get a very good feel for these individuals. Does that make sense? Does it even answer your question? Sometimes I don't know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually like that it doesn't have something scary at the end, but I am a total sucker for the feels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *cough* *puts on Mod Pants and No Fun Hat* Reminder that downvoting because of who the author is (as opposed to what they've written) counts as malicious downvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-12345-J - The Littlest Cancer Patient
Anomalous properties include:

  • Talking excitedly about their next birthday, which will never actually occur.
  • Asking why daddy looks so sad, even if SCP-12345-J cannot directly see its father
  • Inability to comprehend death, SCP-12345 repeatedly asks to see Scruffy, the family puppy, even though SCP-12345-J had already been told that Scruffy had to be put down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You did ask me to read it over, but it really wouldn't have mattered — it's not the kind of thing you could have just tweaked prior to posting.

And no problem :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry I can't help it I guess ;A;

But yeah, point taken.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yet they're trees that love, Zyn. And a tear falls upon my keyboard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damnit esko I wanted dibs on this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was kinda referring to when it was still in the draft form. I vaguely remember asking you to read over it while Roget and I were still doing rather major changes, but I definitely could be wrong (I asked quite a few people. :/)

I don't really have any suggestions for improving it. The notes are not in a position to add to the SCP's emotional impact because they are not a natural extension of the article.

Gotcha. Thanks for all your feedback, Scanny. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, well in that case—

DIBS

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not necessarily good that I could expect your next SCP to be "lava lamp where the lava forms into the face of the last person you wronged, and it speaks to you in whalesong (reminding you that they're endangered and making the creys happen) and your brain anomalously decrypts it into the thing they were thinking when you wronged them, in cinquain form".

I think a joke SCP that is trying OTT-hard to give you feels could be funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Potential spoilers
hide

…actually I threw that in as a means to indicate that the mother and the father were both trees. "Steadfast" and "changed with the seasons" was to give a rough idea of which kinds of trees.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like we've had far too many objects like this lately.

Mind giving me some examples so I know what to avoid in the future?

Then you go and tack on some behind-the-scenes at MC&D, which makes this article feel like it was meant entirely as a vehicle for that.

The MC&D angle included more writing on Roget's part than mine, and while I can't speak for him regarding that, I personally thought it added to the article.

Could you offer suggestions as to how to make it seem less "tacked-on"?

I feel like I'm being accusatory, so if I'm coming off as that, I'm sorry. I really do appreciate your detailed feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1338: I don’t think he would want to. The only reason I’m here is because my mother wanted to tell my father something, and I was the one who passed the message. She said that he was so steadfast, while she was fickle and changed with the seasons. But he loved her for that anyway, and that was what made her love him.

noep no feels here

do you see any feels

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
but you're dangerously close to being typecast as "the feels author".

You might want to try something a little different for your next one, is alls I'm sayin'.

I'll try. (Though I will state that both my first (1355) and most recent article that is not a collab (1338) are not feels-oriented.)

Edit: Parentheseesss d:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Scantron's point isn't completely lost, Zyn. This one is good enough to upvote (to me) and your others have been too, but you're dangerously close to being typecast as "the feels author". You write A Thing + feels. It's not necessarily good that I could expect your next SCP to be "lava lamp where the lava forms into the face of the last person you wronged, and it speaks to you in whalesong (reminding you that they're endangered and making the creys happen) and your brain anomalously decrypts it into the thing they were thinking when you wronged them, in cinquain form".

You might want to try something a little different for your next one, is alls I'm sayin'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was actually quite awake and feeling pretty good when I read it — I didn't bother reading at all last night when I was in a funk.

I don't really have any suggestions for improving it. The notes are not in a position to add to the SCP's emotional impact because they are not a natural extension of the article. Thus, you'd have to change the SCP itself, and I wouldn't know what to add there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Scan, you are a colossal pedant. That is why I love you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like we've had far too many objects like this lately. Dredging up cherished memories seems to be the new skeleton jumping out, and this one really doesn't offer anything new or intriguing to the pool. It's kind of like the bowl of soup, except it works when it's most needed, rather than when it's used. Then you go and tack on some behind-the-scenes at MC&D, which makes this article feel like it was meant entirely as a vehicle for that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do quite like this this.

It's simple, straightforward, weaves into the MC&D group, and is probably the best non-horror new SCP I've seen up here in a dog's age.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: New SCP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Regarding your question
Yes.

The stories behind those objects are up to your interpretation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Roget and I were discussing the potential security risks of this SCP. Out of curiosity, can you think of any? There's a pretty straightforward one that Flames mentioned during the editing process, but because this is a Foundation document, it's not included.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is what happens when you read blogs and are too tired to look of an SCP, Scanny. d: That said, can you offer any suggestions regarding how to improve this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Exactly this. It feels like it's trying too hard.

How does one have a "trying too hard"?- Scantron

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just a question regarding the last addendum.

Is it referring to...
Marshall, Carter, and Dark themselves?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After some deliberation, I decided to upvote.
While this doesn't do much to me, I have a soft spot for mundane anomalies with an origin story - in this case, another unmarketable product (after the reverse mirror voodoo doll) sitting in MC&D storage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This did pretty much nothing for me. And the notes pretty much screamed "THIS IS VAGUE BUT IT IS REFERRING TO SOMETHING BAD PLS FEEL EMOTINGS" at me. So that goes to a solid downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You managed to make it emotional without it being blatant "YOU SHOULD FEEL SAD NOW" writing, well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The problem with scips (or stories in general) that try to go for an emotional reaction is that they tend to focus too much on that and not enough on making the scip (or story in general) particularly interesting. And since I don't actually feel anything while reading this, downvote.

Yes, I am a massive hypocrite :I

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I saw simple everyday object that grabs RIGHT at the heart-strings and pulls 'em, and knew this was by Zyn. I saw "Wehrner" and knew it was Roget. Congrats on having signature styles. :-P

That said, I enjoyed this quite a bit. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
collab between Zyn and Roget

how do I upvote and downvote simultaneously

not like a neutral vote, like a Schrodinger's vote that exists but cannot be determined until you look at it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Precisely! This leans a little more towards the willfulness in such scenarios, though. Memories are one thing, but tangible proof can be reassuring.

I'm glad you enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Been thinking a lot about memory related SCPs recently and I enjoy this one. Of course we like to think we don't but we all remember past events with little changes, so they're a bit more like what we wished they were.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In other news, Zyn is ruining my life with her emotional skips. Back to you, Silber.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Informal studies show that Zyn is the leading cause of people using words like "personal", "bittersweet", and "feels" in SCP comments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am made of feels.

Also Roget is an excellent collab partner.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hope y'all enjoy the article. We worked pretty hard on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know my soft spot for these, Zyn… Why you gotta poke 'em…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with that, bbaztek. Not everything has to be all dire uber-keter, after all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCPs that tell small, personal stories are the best. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Roget suggested a wallet SCP to me one day when I happened to be ranting about losing something. This has been stewing for a few weeks and has gone through several changes from the original idea.

Many thanks to Troy for inspiration, Vezaz, Woggle, Silber, Flames, and Dmatix for reviewing this during the writing stages, and Azzle for looking over the near-finished product. Apologies to anyone I didn’t address, and of course, thank you so much to Roget for cheering me up that day and spearheading this article.

e: BECAUSE CAPS

ETA: noting that I've added a new image in, since I can't confirm that the old one is compliant with Creative Commons. New image is from here: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Leather_wallets_09.JPG
